title: Ulric Dunmar
content:
Ulric Dunmar (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as captain of the Mossenden Waves when they won the interregional playoff crown in 1862.

Early life and college career.

Ulric Dunmar played college basketball in Glimmerford before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as captain.

Professional career.

Ulric Dunmar joined the Mossenden Waves and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1857s. By 1861, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the interregional playoff crown, Ulric Dunmar remained with the Mossenden Waves for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Personal life.

Ulric Dunmar retired to Glimmerford and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1864.

Championship season.

The 1862 interregional playoff crown campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Ulric Dunmar averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Glimmerford Memorial Arena in Glimmerford.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Ulric Dunmar recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1862 playoffs.

Awards and honors.

Following the interregional playoff crown, Ulric Dunmar received Finals Most Valuable Player recognition and was named to the All-League first team.

Playing style.

As captain, Ulric Dunmar preferred structured half-court sets and emphasized defensive rotations. Teammates credited his communication when the Mossenden Waves switched to zone coverage late in quarters.

Legacy.

Historians of the Mossenden Waves cite Ulric Dunmar's 1862 run as the turning point that restored attendance at Glimmerford Memorial Arena. A commemorative banner with his name hangs above the home bench.
